explanation: The natural language statement requires that the patient is never connected to the breathing circuit during the system's start-up and self-test phases. The `StartUpMode` atomic proposition represents the system being in its start-up phase, and `SelfTestMode` represents the system being in its self-test phase. The `patientConnected` atomic proposition indicates when the patient is connected. The LTL formula `G ((StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) -> !patientConnected)` captures this by stating that 'Globally (G), if the system is in `StartUpMode` OR (`|`) `SelfTestMode`, then (->) it must NOT (`!`) be the case that the `patientConnected`.' This ensures the patient is never connected during these specified initial phases.

This LTL property states that globally (G), it must always be the case that the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit (`!patientConnected`) if the system is either in `StartUpMode` or `SelfTestMode`. The natural language phrase "when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test" is captured by the disjunction (`|`) of `StartUpMode` and `SelfTestMode`, as these modes inherently imply the system is powered on and represent the period of start-up and self-test. The phrase "shall not be connected" translates to a global negation (`G` and `!patientConnected`) conditioned on these modes. Therefore, the property ensures that the condition of a patient being connected never co-occurs with the system being in either its start-up or self-test phase.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then the 'patientConnected' atomic proposition must be false. This directly translates the requirement that the patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit ('!patientConnected') when the system is undergoing its start-up and self-test phases ('StartUpMode | SelfTestMode'). The 'G' operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times throughout the system's operation, encompassing all instances of start-up and self-test.

The natural language statement "The patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test" implies that at any point in time, if the system is in either the 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then the 'patientConnected' state must be false. The 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode' collectively represent the system being 'powered on and through start-up and self-test' as described in their definitions. The LTL formula `G ( (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) -> !patientConnected )` captures this by stating that Globally (G), it must always be the case that if the system is in 'StartUpMode' OR 'SelfTestMode', then it is NOT true that the 'patientConnected' to the breathing circuit.

The natural language statement requires that the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit whenever the system is undergoing its start-up and self-test procedures. This is a global property, meaning it must hold at all times. The LTL formula captures this by stating that globally (G), if the system is in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then it must be true that the patient is NOT connected ('!patientConnected'). The 'powered on' part is implicitly covered by the system being in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', as these modes only occur when the system is powered.

The LTL property states that globally (G), it must always be true that if the system is in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then the 'patientConnected' atomic proposition must be false. This precisely captures the requirement that the patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit during the system's start-up and self-test phases.

The natural language statement requires that the patient is never connected to the breathing circuit while the system is undergoing its start-up procedure, which includes both 'Start-up Mode' and 'SelfTest Mode'. The LTL formula captures this by stating that globally (G), if the system is in either 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then it must always be the case that the 'patientConnected' proposition is false (!patientConnected).

The natural language statement requires that the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit during specific operational phases: when the system is powered on and going through its start-up and self-test procedures. The atomic propositions `StartUpMode` and `SelfTestMode` directly represent these phases. Being in either of these modes implies the system is powered on and undergoing initial setup. Therefore, the property states that globally (G), if the system is in `StartUpMode` or `SelfTestMode`, then the `patientConnected` proposition must be false (!patientConnected). The 'or' ( | ) operator is used because both modes are part of the initial boot sequence where the patient should not be connected. The implication (->) ensures that this condition is enforced only when the system is in these specified modes.

The natural language statement specifies a condition under which the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit. This condition is described as "when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test". The atomic propositions 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode' directly represent these specific phases of the system's operation. These modes implicitly occur only when the system is powered on, making an explicit 'powered on' proposition redundant for defining this specific interval. Therefore, the LTL property states that globally, if the system is in either 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode', then the 'patientConnected' proposition must be false, ensuring the patient is not connected during these critical initialisation and testing phases.

The natural language statement requires that the patient is never connected to the breathing circuit during the system's start-up and self-test phases. 'StartUpMode' indicates the system initialization, and 'SelfTestMode' indicates the self-test procedure. Both modes are part of the initial process when the system is powered on and preparing for use. The condition 'when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test' is captured by the disjunction of these two modes: 'StartUpMode | SelfTestMode'. The requirement 'patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit' is directly translated to '!patientConnected'. The LTL operator 'G' (globally) ensures that this condition holds true at all times. Therefore, the LTL formula 'G((StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) -> !patientConnected)' expresses that it is globally true that if the system is in Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode, then the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit.
